tasks.title: WAVE04 Database: Harden the runtime DB ownership seam and reduce process-global fallback
tasks.prompt: Use `lib/design/design-plan.json`, `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/04-database-layer.md`, and `../socialpredict/README/PRODUCTION-NOTES/BACKEND/12-database-caching.md` as the binding design context for WAVE04 database runtime ownership. Complete the first useful slice by hardening `backend/internal/app/runtime/db.go` as the owned runtime DB seam: make the minimum production contract for pool sizing, connection lifetime, SSL/TLS posture, and shutdown or health hooks explicit, and reduce `SetDB`/`GetDB` so the production startup path no longer relies on process-global fallback state. Preserve current runtime behavior where safe, keep the change reversible, and do not start caching or Redis work.
acceptance_criteria: The touched runtime DB path makes its minimum production ownership explicit for pool sizing, connection lifetime, SSL/TLS posture, and shutdown or health integration.
The production startup path no longer depends on `SetDB`/`GetDB` as a hidden runtime fallback; if compatibility remains, it is isolated to tests or a narrow migration bridge.
Focused tests cover the hardened runtime DB seam deterministically for the touched behavior.

focus: explicit pool, SSL/TLS, and lifecycle contract for the touched path

notes: Keep Postgres as the authoritative system of record.
Do not treat caching, Redis, or a generic database service as part of this slice.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `700db0a4-da61-4d6f-883a-4bf6d1d5b468`  
task_id: `WAVE04-DB-001`  
status: `complete`

Changed `internal/app/runtime/db.go`, `internal/app/runtime/db_test.go`, and `main.go`.

Runtime DB ownership is now explicit for pool sizing, connection lifetime, SSL/TLS posture, readiness pinging, and shutdown. `InitDB` now returns/configures the explicit handle without mutating `SetDB`/`GetDB`; those globals remain documented as legacy/test bridge only. `main.go` now closes the DB pool after server shutdown.

Assumption: production runtime should fail closed on TLS posture when `APP_ENV=production` unless SSL mode is set to `require`, `verify-ca`, or `verify-full`, or TLS requirement is explicitly disabled.

Checks run:
- `go test ./internal/app/runtime -count=1`
- runtime reliability suite, including two deterministic runtime test runs
- `go vet ./...`
- architecture boundary checks
- quality guardrails
- `gofmt`
- `git diff --check`

follow_ups: none.

tasks.title: WAVE04 Database: Tighten migration failure posture and introduce a single-writer startup seam
tasks.prompt: After the runtime DB seam is explicit, tighten the startup mutation path. Replace warning-only or every-replica default behavior around migrations and any still-startup-owned seed writes with an explicit startup-writer seam that preserves the current repo shape. Choose the smallest reversible enforcement the backend can support now, but make it impossible for request-serving startup to treat shared schema or seed mutation as an implicit every-process responsibility. Fail closed or remain unready on unsafe schema state, and keep caching or Redis out of scope.
acceptance_criteria: Touched startup code no longer treats migrations or any still-startup-owned seed writes as implicit every-replica work.
Migration or schema-incompatibility failure in the touched startup path no longer degrades to warning-only continuation; the process fails closed or remains unready.
Focused tests cover the chosen startup-writer gate and migration failure posture deterministically for the touched path.

focus: single-writer startup seam for migrations and startup-owned seeds
deterministic migration and startup safety checks

notes: The enforcement mechanism may be leader-only mode, a dedicated startup-writer mode, or a DB-backed serialization seam, but it must be explicit.
Do not leave warning-only migration failure or `AutoMigrate` fallback as the production end state.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `63c28c0d-db5e-4036-a78d-8f2953ead2be`  
task_id: `WAVE04-DB-002`  
status: `completed`

Spawned `software-action-dispatcher-agent` first as requested; it coordinated specialists and the final report was updated.

files_changed:
- `backend/main.go`
- `backend/startup_mutation.go`
- `backend/startup_mutation_test.go`
- `backend/migration/migrate.go`
- `backend/migration/migrate_test.go`
- `backend/internal/app/runtime/startup_mutation.go`
- `backend/internal/app/runtime/startup_mutation_test.go`

What changed:
- Added explicit `STARTUP_WRITER` startup mutation gate.
- Writer processes run migrations plus startup-owned seeds.
- Non-writer processes only verify applied migrations without mutating schema.
- Removed warning-only migration continuation and `AutoMigrate` fallback.
- Added focused tests for writer/non-writer branching, migration failure abort, env parsing, and read-only migration verification.

checks_run:
- `go test -count=1 . ./migration ./internal/app/runtime` passed twice with workspace-local Go caches.
- `go test -count=1 ./...` passed.
- `go vet ./...` passed.
- `gofmt` wrapper passed.
- architecture boundary check passed.

Assumption:
- Exactly one deployed startup writer will run with `STARTUP_WRITER=true`; request-serving replicas remain unset or false.

follow_ups:
- Deploy/configure the single startup writer role before relying on non-writer request-serving startup.

tasks.title: WAVE04 Database: Define DB-backed readiness and liveness for serving traffic
tasks.prompt: With startup mutation policy tightened, make the serving contract reflect real database condition. Replace hard-coded health success with explicit DB-backed readiness and liveness behavior, or equivalent owned runtime signals, that distinguish process aliveness from safe request-serving readiness after startup. Reuse the runtime seam, keep `/v0/system/metrics` separate from health probes, and avoid a broad observability redesign.
acceptance_criteria: Touched runtime or server code distinguishes process liveness from DB-backed readiness instead of reporting unconditional success.
The readiness decision for the touched path is tied to owned runtime DB condition rather than ad hoc handler behavior or application metrics routes.
Focused route and runtime tests cover the touched readiness and liveness behavior deterministically.

focus: separation of health probes from application metrics

notes: Keep health and readiness operator-facing and separate from application metrics.
Do not reintroduce a hard-coded `ok` contract once DB condition is available.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `ccfc2ea5-2bbd-4ee7-9205-4c59cec97f7d`

- [serving_probe.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app/runtime/serving_probe.go): added owned runtime liveness/readiness probe.
- [serving_probe_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/internal/app/runtime/serving_probe_test.go): added deterministic runtime probe tests.
- [server_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/server/server_test.go) and [server_contract_test.go](/workspace/socialpredict/backend/server/server_contract_test.go): updated probe contract assertions.

Checks run:

- `run_reliability_suite.sh /workspace/socialpredict ./server` passed.
- `run_reliability_suite.sh /workspace/socialpredict ./internal/app/runtime` passed.

Assumption: existing dirty startup/migration/runtime DB files were pre-existing and left intact.  
follow_ups: `None`

tasks.title: WAVE04 Database: Remove the next raw request-boundary DB leaks behind owned seams
tasks.prompt: After the runtime and readiness story is explicit, migrate the next raw request-boundary `*gorm.DB` call sites onto owned service or repository seams. Start with the concrete remaining leaks called out by the design context, especially stats, admin add-user, and any adjacent route wiring that still passes raw DB handles into handlers. Preserve endpoint behavior, keep handlers thin, and do not solve this by inventing a new central DAL or generic CRUD facade.
acceptance_criteria: The touched request-boundary handlers no longer take raw `*gorm.DB` when an owned service or repository seam is sufficient.
Touched server or container wiring passes explicit owned dependencies instead of raw DB handles for the migrated paths.
Focused tests cover the migrated handler behavior or service seams deterministically for the touched slice.

focus: reuse of existing service and repository seams
avoidance of DAL or generic CRUD regression

out_of_scope: legacy-model decoupling beyond the touched slice
notes: Prefer the smallest vertical slice that removes a real raw DB leak.
Do not move persistence shaping back into handlers.

tasks.title: WAVE04 Database: Make bet placement atomic and stop-and-review the remaining accounting surface
tasks.prompt: Finish WAVE04 by turning one high-risk accounting-sensitive workflow into an explicit transaction-scoped unit of work, starting with bet placement unless the touched code reveals an even smaller live compensation path. Define which writes must commit together, where transaction scope begins and ends across repositories, and what primary-DB concurrency protection the touched workflow needs. Preserve integer-first accounting behavior, keep automatic retry or circuit-breaker behavior out of scope, and end the wave with a precise inventory of the remaining sell-position or market-resolution transaction surface while keeping caching or Redis explicitly deferred.
acceptance_criteria: One high-risk accounting-sensitive workflow in the touched code is converted from compensation-style behavior to an explicit transaction-scoped unit of work with clear commit boundaries.
The touched workflow makes its primary-DB concurrency protection explicit and preserves existing accounting behavior without adding generic automatic retry or circuit-breaker behavior.

Focused tests prove atomicity-sensitive behavior for the migrated workflow, and the wave ends with an explicit remaining inventory for other accounting-sensitive flows while keeping caching or Redis deferred.

focus: explicit atomic unit of work for the first accounting-sensitive workflow
deterministic evidence for touched transaction and concurrency behavior
precise remaining accounting-surface inventory with caching still deferred

out_of_scope: a generic transaction framework for every workflow
automatic retry or circuit-breaker rollout around money-moving writes
notes: Start with the smallest high-risk workflow, expected to be bet placement.
Keep Redis and caching explicitly deferred until runtime ownership and accounting correctness are stronger.
tasks.last_summary:
task_uid: `d439e58f-93be-42de-bd8c-1cb05cab0705`  
task_id: `WAVE04-DB-005`  
status: `completed`

Spawned `software-action-dispatcher-agent`; it coordinated specialist review and verifier sign-off.

Changed bet placement to require an explicit transaction unit of work. The GORM adapter now scopes user balance reads/debits and bet insertion to one transaction, with Postgres `FOR UPDATE` protection on the user row. Removed the buy-side compensation ledger path from the active placement workflow and added rollback coverage for failed bet creation.
